content: By 2025, the recursive self-improving memory architecture at the core of autonomous self-supervised learning agents can be reframed as a nested agent ecosystem, where each self-supervised data exploration step is driven by a hierarchy of sub-agents with individually calibrated curiosity drives, rather than a single monolithic curiosity objective. This nested structure allows lower-level sub-agents to explore local environmental anomalies while higher-level agents integrate discovered patterns into long-term memory without catastrophic forgetting.
